title: Databricks Data Engineer Knowledge Test
description: Test your understanding of Databricks Data Engineer with 25 questions.

questions.question: In a Databricks multi-tenant data lake used by Lyft, Snap, and Snowflake, design a real-time Silver layer that ingests streaming JSON events from a cloud bucket into Delta Lake, performs per-tenant feature extraction, and materializes a Gold table used by dashboards. Outline per-tenant isolation, schema drift handling with schema evolution policy, low-latency upserts into Gold, Unity Catalog-based masking, a streaming fault-tolerance plan for late data, and a test strategy with synthetic late-arriving events?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests real-time data architecture, multi-tenant governance, and fault tolerance with late data in a production Databricks workflow.

## Key Concepts
- Real-time streaming with Auto Loader and Structured Streaming
- Multi-tenant isolation via Unity Catalog schemas and RBAC
- Schema drift handling with evolution policies
- CDC-like upserts into Gold with MERGE on event_id
- Masking via secure views and Unity Catalog masking policies
- Watermarks and late-data handling; data-quality gates
- Synthetic late-data tests and end-to-end validation

## Code Example
```sql
-- Example MERGE into Gold (simplified)
MERGE INTO gold_table AS g
USING silver_stage AS s
ON g.event_id = s.event_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET g.value = s.value, g.updated_at = current_timestamp()
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (event_id, tenant_id, value, updated_at) VALUES (s.event_id, s.tenant_id, s.value, current_timestamp())
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you monitor per-tenant latency and data skew, and which metrics alert you? 
- What are the trade-offs of per-tenant schemas vs a shared schema with masking layers?

Add a per-file AuditLineage table recording path, ingest_ts, file_size, source_name. Implement Unity Catalog RBAC for Silver and AuditLineage. Outline a lightweight synthetic-data test plan to verify CDC, watermark, schema-evolution, and RBAC?

Use Delta Live Tables to build Silver from Bronze. Define Silver with event_id, event_time, payload, and a provenance column; populate a separate AuditFiles Delta table via a lightweight batch_id UDF

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests end-to-end design for multi-source ingestion, cross-source dedup, and governance in Databricks, plus practical test planning.

## Key Concepts
- Auto Loader and structured streaming
- Delta Lake CDC MERGE
- Watermarking and schema evolution
- Unity Catalog RBAC and data lineage
- Synthetic-data tests

## Code Example
```javascript
// Pseudo-Delta MERGE for CDC
spark.sql(`MERGE INTO silver_table AS s
USING bronze_table AS b
ON s.canonical_id = b.canonical_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET s.* = b.*
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *`)
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you implement schema drift handling across sources?
- What metrics would you surface in a data quality dashboard?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests practical, beginner-friendly Delta Lake patterns: per-file provenance, MERGE upserts, late data handling, and Unity Catalog RBAC with tenant-scoped views. It also probes test design for synthetic data scenarios.

## Key Concepts
- Auto Loader for incremental loading and provenance
- MERGE-based upserts to simulate CDC
- Watermarks to handle late data
- Unity Catalog RBAC and per-tenant views
- Lightweight data-quality scoring and test harness

## Code Example
```sql
-- MERGE into Silver (example)
MERGE INTO Silver AS s
USING Bronze AS b
ON s.event_id = b.event_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET s.col = b.col
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (...);
```

```sql
-- Simple quality_score UDF sketch (conceptual)
CREATE FUNCTION quality_score(event_time TIMESTAMP, value DOUBLE) RETURNS INT
LANGUAGE SQL
AS
  'CASE WHEN event_time IS NULL OR value IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 100 END';
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test idempotence when the same file is ingested twice?
- How would you extend the approach for additional tenants or data domains?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design near-real-time enrichment, multi-source joins, and robust handling of outages in an established Databricks data platform.

## Key Concepts
- Delta Live Tables streaming joins between Bronze and a minute-delayed enrichment feed
- Idempotent MERGE upserts into Gold for deterministic updates
- Per-record provenance (source_path, file_timestamp, ingest_ts) preserved in Silver/ Audit
- Schema evolution policy for nested JSON and Parquet
- Unity Catalog RBAC masking for PII fields in downstream
- Late data handling with watermarking and optional default enrichment
- End-to-end test plan including enrichment outages and delayed events

## Code Example
```sql
-- Pseudo MERGE into Gold after enrichment
MERGE INTO gold AS g
USING silver_enriched AS s
ON g.key = s.key
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET g.value = s.value, g.enrichment_ts = s.enrichment_ts
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (key, value, enrichment_ts) VALUES (s.key, s.value, s.enrichment_ts);
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you monitor enrichment latency and automatically raise alerts on degradation?
- How would you simulate enrichment service outages in CI/CD tests while preserving idempotency?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Assesses practical multi-tenant data warehousing skills, including SCD2 history management, data provenance, late data handling, and governance in Databricks and Delta Lake.

## Key Concepts
- Slowly Changing Dimensions Type 2 with surrogate keys preserves historical customer records while tracking current state.
- MERGE upserts with event-time watermarking ensure accurate processing of out-of-order and late-arriving data.
- Per-file provenance and an AuditFiles lineage table enable end-to-end traceability of data assets.
- Delta Lake history and file-level metadata support compliance and debugging workflows.
- Unity Catalog RBAC enforces fine-grained data access controls across workspaces and catalogs.

## Code Example
```sql
-- SCD Type 2 MERGE into Silver
MERGE INTO silver.customer_dim AS s
USING (
  SELECT c.*, sha2(concat(customer_id, effective_date), 256) AS customer_sk
  FROM bronze.customer_staging c
) AS src
ON s.customer_id = src.customer_id AND s.is_current = true
WHEN MATCHED AND s.record_hash != src.record_hash THEN
  UPDATE SET s.is_current = false, s.validity_end = src.effective_date
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
  INSERT (customer_sk, customer_id, record_hash, name, email, effective_date, validity_start, validity_end, is_current)
  VALUES (src.customer_sk, src.customer_id, src.record_hash, src.name, src.email, src.effective_date, src.effective_date, null, true);

-- Late-arrival handling via watermark
CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP VIEW late_arrivals AS
SELECT * FROM bronze.customer_staging
WHERE ingestion_time > (SELECT MAX(validity_end) FROM silver.customer_dim WHERE customer_id = staging.customer_id);

-- Audit trail for lineage
INSERT INTO silver.audit_files (file_path, record_count, ingestion_time, source_system)
SELECT input_file_name(), count(*), current_timestamp(), 's3_log_source'
FROM bronze.customer_staging
GROUP BY input_file_name();

-- Gold layer MERGE upsert
MERGE INTO gold.customer_master AS g
USING silver.customer_dim AS s
ON g.customer_sk = s.customer_sk AND s.is_current = true
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *;
```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question tests end-to-end lakehouse design: ingestion configuration, schema evolution, CDC upserts, lineage auditing, access control, quality gates, and late-data handling at scale.

## Key Concepts
- Auto Loader with fileMetadata and schema evolution
- Tenant-scoped MERGE CDC into partitioned Gold
- Per-tenant lineage tracking and RBAC masking
- Data quality gates with quarantine and downstream blocking
- Watermarking for late data and partial-file handling

## Code Example
```python
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, current_timestamp, md5

# Auto Loader with fileMetadata for lineage
df = (spark.readStream
  .format("cloudFiles")
  .option("cloudFiles.format", "parquet")
  .option("cloudFiles.useFileMetadata", "true")
  .schema(target_schema)
  .load("s3://bucket/raw/"))

# Extract lineage per file
lineage_df = df.select(
    col("_metadata.file_path").alias("source_path"),
    col("_metadata.file_size").alias("file_size_bytes"),
    current_timestamp().alias("ingestion_timestamp")
).dropDuplicates(["source_path"])

# Write lineage to Silver table
(lineage_df.writeStream
  .option("checkpointLocation", "/mnt/lineage/chk")
  .trigger(availableNow=True)
  .toTable("catalog.bronze.lineage"))

# Watermark and MERGE for Gold table
from pyspark.sql.functions import window

watermarked = df.withWatermark("event_timestamp", "15 minutes")

def upsert_to_gold(microbatch_df, batch_id):
    microbatch_df.createOrReplaceTempView("updates")
    microbatch_df._jdf.sparkSession().sql("""
        MERGE INTO catalog.gold.events t
        USING updates s
        ON t.tenant_id = s.tenant_id 
           AND t.event_id = s.event_id 
           AND t.date = s.date
        WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET *
        WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *
    """)

(watermarked.writeStream
  .foreachBatch(upsert_to_gold)
  .option("checkpointLocation", "/mnt/gold/chk")
  .trigger(availableNow=True)
  .start())
```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

This question validates your ability to design a robust, scalable multi-tenant ingestion pipeline that guarantees idempotent writes, maintains strong data provenance, and enforces governance while handling schema evolution and late-arriving data.

## Key Concepts

- **Exact Deduplication**: Using a composite key (tenant_id, event_id) with MERGE-based upserts ensures idempotent writes across pipeline replays, handling both duplicates and late-arriving records gracefully.
- **Provenance Tracking**: Preserving per-file metadata in an audit trail enables compliance auditing, root cause analysis, and data lineage tracking.
- **Schema Drift**: Implementing explicit evolution policies for nested JSON structures prevents pipeline failures when source schemas change while maintaining data integrity.
- **Gold Layer Partitioning**: Partitioning by tenant_id and date optimizes multi-tenant BI queries and enables efficient time-based filters.
- **Data Quality Gates**: Automated checks on deduplication rates and non-null constraints catch issues early and provide visibility into data health.
- **Fault Tolerance**: Auto Loader with checkpointing ensures exactly-once processing, resumability after failures, and integration with Unity Catalog for governance.

Outline concrete steps, trade-offs, and how you would monitor and roll back if the contract changes unexpectedly?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question tests the ability to design enterprise-grade data pipeline governance in a multi-tenant lakehouse environment. It validates understanding of Databricks DLT, data contracts, schema drift handling, and operational safety mechanisms.

## Key Concepts
- **Per-tenant data contracts**: Centralized schema definition stored in Delta tables
- **UDF-driven projection**: Custom logic to enforce contract boundaries
- **Quarantine pattern**: Isolating non-conforming records for investigation
- **Schema evolution governance**: Controlled field addition with approval workflows
- **CDC upserts**: Merge semantics preserving change history
- **Unity Catalog RBAC**: Fine-grained access control with column masking
- **Watermarks**: Handling late-arriving data with configurable windows

## Trade-offs
| Aspect | Consideration |
|--------|---------------|
| UDF vs. Native Projection | UDFs offer flexibility but impact performance; consider Spark native projection for high-throughput paths |
| Auto-evolution vs. Manual Approval | Auto-update reduces latency but risks propagating bad schemas; governance queues provide safety net |
| Quarantine Storage | Balancing cost vs. observability—retain quarantine data for SLA-defined periods |

## Monitoring & Observability
- **Pipeline health**: DLT pipeline metrics via Databricks SDK
- **Contract drift alerts**: Stream quality expectations failure rates
- **Quarantine metrics**: Track quarantine volume by tenant and field
- **Rollback automation**: CI/CD pipeline integration with contract version tags

## Code Example
```python
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, to_json, from_json
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType

def project_to_contract(df, contract_schema):
    """Project DataFrame to contract schema, quarantine unknown fields"""
    contract_fields = set(contract_schema.fieldNames())
    
    selected = [c for c in df.columns if c in contract_fields]
    quarantine = [c for c in df.columns if c not in contract_fields]
    
    return df.select(*selected).withColumn("_quarantined_fields", to_json(struct(*quarantine)))

# DLT Pipeline Definition
@dlt.table
def silver_tenant_data():
    contract = spark.read.table("catalog.governance.contracts").filter(col("tenant_id") == current_tenant_id)
    
    return (
        dlt.read_stream("bronze_ingestion")
        .transform(project_to_contract, contract)
        .filter("_quarantined_fields IS NULL")
    )

# Quality Gates
@dlt.expect("valid_contract_fields", "_quarantined_fields IS NULL")
@dlt.expect("no_null_keys", "tenant_id IS NOT NULL AND record_id IS NOT NULL")
```

## Why This Is Asked

```sql
ON g.record_hash = s.record_hash

WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ...;
```

questions.explanation:
Why This Is Asked
- Tests end-to-end Databricks pipeline design: provenance, schema drift handling, CDC-style upserts, RBAC, data quality, late data, and testing.

Key Concepts
- Delta Live Tables, per-file provenance, and audit tables
- Schema drift handling for semi-structured JSON
- MERGE-based CDC into Silver
- Star/denormalized Gold for dashboards
- Unity Catalog RBAC and masking views
- Data-quality gates and late-data watermark
- Synthetic data testing and fault tolerance

Code Example
```python
# placeholder UDF sketch for provenance
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf, struct
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, TimestampType

@udf(returnType=StructType([StructField('source_path', StringType()),
                          StructField('file_timestamp', TimestampType()),
                          StructField('ingest_ts', TimestampType())]))
def provenance(source_path, file_ts, ingest_ts):
    return {'source_path': source_path, 'file_timestamp': file_ts, 'ingest_ts': ingest_ts}
```

Follow-up Questions
- How would you test schema drift handling and verification of lineage across tenant boundaries?
- What monitoring would you add to detect spikes in late data and correlate with file-level provenance?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Evaluates lifecycle design for multi-tenant data, balancing cost, accessibility, and governance; tests whether the candidate can implement per-tenant policies, archival workflows, and PIT access without breaking dashboards.

## Key Concepts

- Per-tenant retention metadata
- Archival vs. purging strategy
- Delta Time Travel accessibility
- VACUUM and data gravity considerations
- Unity Catalog RBAC and masking implications

## Code Example

```sql
-- Archive older per-tenant data
INSERT INTO archive.bronze_archive
SELECT * FROM bronze
WHERE date < DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL 365 DAY);

-- Purge from main Bronze table
VACUUM bronze RETAIN 365 DAYS;
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you handle legal holds that override retention windows?
- How would you test performance and ensure dashboards reflect current data correctly?
